Apple___Apple_scab.first_aid: Remove and destroy infected leaves immediately. Do NOT compost infected material. Apply copper-based fungicide spray within 24 hours.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove all visibly infected leaves and fruits from the tree
Step 2: Spray Captan or Mancozeb fungicide (2g/L water) every 7–10 days
Step 3: Improve air circulation by pruning dense branches
Step 4: Avoid overhead watering — switch to drip irrigation
Step 5: After harvest, rake and burn all fallen leaves
Step 6: Apply dormant copper spray before next season bud break

Apple___Black_rot.first_aid: Immediately prune and burn all infected branches and mummified fruits. Apply thiophanate-methyl fungicide.
action_plan: Step 1: Prune all dead wood and cankers with sterilized tools
Step 2: Remove all mummified fruits still hanging on tree
Step 3: Apply Thiophanate-methyl (1.5g/L) spray every 10 days
Step 4: Ensure proper fertilization — avoid high nitrogen
Step 5: Monitor for fire blight as secondary infection risk
Step 6: Apply bordeaux mixture as protective winter spray

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.first_aid: Apply myclobutanil fungicide immediately. Remove nearby juniper/cedar trees which are alternate hosts.
action_plan: Step 1: Spray Myclobutanil (Nova) at first sign of orange spore masses
Step 2: Identify and remove nearby red cedar / juniper trees if possible
Step 3: Apply fungicide every 7–10 days during wet spring weather
Step 4: Plant resistant apple varieties in future
Step 5: Monitor weather — rust spreads rapidly during rainy periods

Apple___healthy.first_aid: No treatment needed. Plant is healthy!
action_plan: Continue regular monitoring (weekly inspection)
Maintain proper irrigation and fertilization schedule
Apply preventive copper spray before monsoon season
Prune for good air circulation annually

Blueberry___healthy.first_aid: No treatment needed. Plant is healthy!
action_plan: Maintain soil pH between 4.5–5.5 for optimal growth
Continue regular monitoring and preventive sprays
Ensure proper mulching to retain moisture

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.first_aid: Spray potassium bicarbonate or sulfur-based fungicide immediately. Remove heavily infected shoots.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove and destroy heavily infected shoots and leaves
Step 2: Apply Wettable Sulfur (2g/L) or Potassium Bicarbonate spray
Step 3: Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization — promotes soft tissue
Step 4: Improve air circulation through pruning
Step 5: Apply spray every 7 days during dry, warm weather
Step 6: Use resistant varieties in future plantings

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.first_aid: No treatment needed. Plant is healthy!
action_plan: Continue weekly monitoring
Apply preventive sulfur spray before flowering
Maintain proper pruning for air circulation

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.first_aid: Apply azoxystrobin or pyraclostrobin fungicide immediately. Ensure proper field drainage.
action_plan: Step 1: Apply Azoxystrobin (Amistar) at first lesion appearance
Step 2: Ensure proper crop rotation — avoid continuous corn planting
Step 3: Improve field drainage to reduce leaf wetness duration
Step 4: Use resistant hybrid varieties next season
Step 5: Apply fungicide at V6 and VT growth stages
Step 6: Destroy infected crop residue after harvest

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.first_aid: Apply propiconazole or mancozeb fungicide. Rust spreads fast — act within 24 hours of detection.
action_plan: Step 1: Apply Propiconazole (Tilt) fungicide immediately at 1ml/L
Step 2: Monitor field daily — rust spreads via wind spores rapidly
Step 3: Avoid late planting which increases rust pressure
Step 4: Use resistant hybrids (check ICAR recommendations)
Step 5: Apply second spray after 10 days if infection persists

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.first_aid: Apply azoxystrobin fungicide. Cigar-shaped lesions indicate active infection — act fast.
action_plan: Step 1: Apply Azoxystrobin + Propiconazole tank mix at first cigar lesion
Step 2: Ensure crop rotation with soybean or wheat next season
Step 3: Remove and destroy infected lower leaves
Step 4: Apply boron + zinc micronutrients to boost plant immunity
Step 5: Scout field every 3–4 days during monsoon season

Corn_(maize)___healthy.first_aid: No treatment needed. Plant is healthy!
action_plan: Continue monitoring every 3–4 days
Apply preventive fungicide at VT stage during monsoon
Ensure proper nitrogen management to avoid lush soft tissue

Grape___Black_rot.first_aid: Apply Myclobutanil or Mancozeb immediately. Remove all mummified berries.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove all mummified berries and infected clusters
Step 2: Apply Myclobutanil (Nova) 10 WP at 1g/L
Step 3: Spray every 7–10 days during wet weather
Step 4: Improve canopy management for better air circulation
Step 5: Apply dormant lime sulfur spray in winter
Step 6: Sanitize vineyard tools between rows

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).display_name: Grape Esca / Black Measles (Wood Disease Complex)
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).first_aid: No curative chemical exists. Remove and burn infected vines immediately to prevent spread.
action_plan: Step 1: Mark and remove all symptomatic vines immediately
Step 2: Burn or deep-bury removed vines — do NOT compost
Step 3: Sterilize pruning tools with 70% alcohol between cuts
Step 4: Apply wound sealant after all pruning cuts
Step 5: Apply arsenite-free wood protectant on pruning wounds
Step 6: Replant with certified disease-free rootstock

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.first_aid: Apply copper-based fungicide. Remove infected leaves before spores spread.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove all infected leaves showing angular brown spots
Step 2: Apply Copper Oxychloride (3g/L) spray
Step 3: Repeat spray after 10 days
Step 4: Improve drainage around vineyard
Step 5: Avoid overhead irrigation

Grape___healthy.first_aid: No treatment needed. Plant is healthy!
action_plan: Continue weekly monitoring
Apply preventive bordeaux mixture before monsoon
Maintain proper canopy management

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).first_aid: No cure. Remove infected trees IMMEDIATELY to prevent spread via psyllid insects. Contact your nearest Krishi Vigyan Kendra.
action_plan: Step 1: Mark and remove infected trees IMMEDIATELY — no cure exists
Step 2: Control Asian Citrus Psyllid with imidacloprid spray (vector control)
Step 3: Do NOT plant new citrus within 50 meters
Step 4: Report to district agriculture office for quarantine assistance
Step 5: Apply reflective mulch to reduce psyllid landing
Step 6: Install yellow sticky traps for psyllid monitoring

Peach___Bacterial_spot.display_name: Peach Bacterial Spot (Xanthomonas arboricola pv. pruni)
Peach___Bacterial_spot.first_aid: Apply copper hydroxide spray immediately. Prune infected shoots during dry weather only.
action_plan: Step 1: Apply Copper Hydroxide (Kocide) at 1.5g/L during dry weather
Step 2: Prune infected twigs 15cm below last visible lesion
Step 3: Sterilize pruning tools between cuts
Step 4: Avoid overhead irrigation — switch to drip
Step 5: Apply oxytetracycline antibiotic spray (if legally permitted in region)
Step 6: Plant resistant varieties in future

Peach___healthy.first_aid: No treatment needed. Plant is healthy!
action_plan: Continue regular monitoring
Apply preventive copper spray before bud break
Thin fruits for better yield quality

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.first_aid: Remove infected plants. Apply copper-based bactericide immediately. Avoid working in wet fields.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove and destroy heavily infected plants
Step 2: Apply Copper Oxychloride (3g/L) or Copper Hydroxide spray
Step 3: Rotate crops — avoid pepper/tomato on same land next season
Step 4: Use certified disease-free seeds
Step 5: Drench soil with Trichoderma viride for root protection
Step 6: Avoid overhead watering

Pepper,_bell___healthy.first_aid: No treatment needed. Plant is healthy!
action_plan: Continue monitoring weekly
Apply preventive copper spray monthly
Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation

Potato___Early_blight.first_aid: Apply Mancozeb or Chlorothalonil fungicide immediately. Remove lower infected leaves.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove all lower leaves with concentric ring spots
Step 2: Apply Mancozeb 75 WP (2.5g/L) spray
Step 3: Repeat spray every 7–10 days
Step 4: Ensure balanced potassium fertilization — K deficiency worsens blight
Step 5: Avoid stress conditions (drought/waterlogging)
Step 6: Use resistant varieties like Kufri Jyoti next season

Potato___Late_blight.display_name: Potato Late Blight (Phytophthora infestans) — HIGH PRIORITY
Potato___Late_blight.first_aid: EMERGENCY: Late blight can destroy entire field in 3–5 days. Apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb within 6 hours. Stop all irrigation immediately.
action_plan: Step 1: STOP IRRIGATION IMMEDIATELY
Step 2: Apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil Gold) at 2.5g/L IMMEDIATELY
Step 3: Remove and burn infected haulms — do NOT compost
Step 4: Spray entire field, not just infected patches
Step 5: Repeat spray every 5 days if wet weather continues
Step 6: Harvest early if >50% foliage infected
Step 7: Report to nearby Krishi Vigyan Kendra for emergency support

Potato___healthy.first_aid: No treatment needed. Plant is healthy!
action_plan: Continue monitoring every 3–4 days during humid weather
Apply preventive Mancozeb spray before monsoon
Ensure proper earthing up and drainage

Raspberry___healthy.first_aid: No treatment needed. Plant is healthy!
action_plan: Continue weekly monitoring
Apply preventive copper spray in spring
Remove old canes after harvest

Soybean___healthy.first_aid: No treatment needed. Plant is healthy!
action_plan: Continue monitoring for soybean rust (Phakopsora pachyrhizi)
Apply preventive fungicide at R1 (flowering) stage
Ensure proper rhizobium inoculation for nitrogen fixation

Squash___Powdery_mildew.first_aid: Apply potassium bicarbonate or neem oil spray immediately. Remove worst infected leaves.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove and destroy heavily infected leaves
Step 2: Apply Potassium Bicarbonate (5g/L) or Neem Oil (5ml/L) spray
Step 3: Avoid overhead watering in the evening
Step 4: Apply spray every 5–7 days
Step 5: Improve plant spacing for air circulation
Step 6: Apply sulfur dust in early morning in severe cases

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.first_aid: Remove infected leaves. Apply Captan or Myclobutanil fungicide spray.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove all leaves showing purple/red spots with scorched edges
Step 2: Apply Captan 50 WP (2g/L) fungicide spray
Step 3: Mulch beds to prevent soil splash inoculation
Step 4: Avoid overhead irrigation
Step 5: Renovate plantings every 3 years to reduce inoculum buildup

Strawberry___healthy.first_aid: No treatment needed. Plant is healthy!
action_plan: Continue monitoring weekly
Apply preventive Captan spray before flowering
Use clean certified runners for new plantings

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.first_aid: Apply copper bactericide immediately. Remove infected leaves and improve ventilation.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove all spotted leaves and water-soaked fruits
Step 2: Apply Copper Hydroxide (Kocide 3000) at 2g/L
Step 3: Alternate with Streptomycin Sulfate every 5 days
Step 4: Avoid working in wet fields to prevent spread
Step 5: Use drip irrigation only
Step 6: Rotate crops next season

Tomato___Early_blight.first_aid: Apply Mancozeb or Chlorothalonil fungicide. Remove lower infected leaves immediately.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove lower leaves showing bull's-eye lesions
Step 2: Apply Mancozeb 75 WP (2.5g/L) spray
Step 3: Mulch around plants to prevent soil splash
Step 4: Repeat spray every 7 days
Step 5: Ensure adequate potassium and calcium nutrition
Step 6: Stake plants for better air circulation

Tomato___Late_blight.first_aid: EMERGENCY: Can destroy field in 3 days. Apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb IMMEDIATELY. Stop all irrigation.
action_plan: Step 1: STOP ALL IRRIGATION
Step 2: Apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil Gold) 2.5g/L IMMEDIATELY
Step 3: Spray entire field — all plants, not just infected ones
Step 4: Remove and burn infected tissue
Step 5: Repeat every 5 days in wet conditions
Step 6: Contact KVK or agricultural extension officer immediately

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.first_aid: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb. Reduce greenhouse humidity by improving ventilation.
action_plan: Step 1: Improve ventilation in greenhouse or polytunnel
Step 2: Reduce humidity below 85% — key control measure
Step 3: Apply Chlorothalonil 75 WP (2g/L) spray
Step 4: Avoid leaf wetness overnight
Step 5: Use resistant varieties with Cf gene

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.first_aid: Remove infected lower leaves. Apply mancozeb or copper fungicide spray immediately.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove all leaves with tiny white spots with dark borders
Step 2: Apply Mancozeb 75 WP (2.5g/L) spray
Step 3: Mulch around base to prevent soil splash
Step 4: Stake plants to improve air circulation
Step 5: Spray every 7–10 days, especially after rain

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.first_aid: Spider mites are NOT fungal — use miticide or neem oil. Do NOT use regular fungicide.
action_plan: Step 1: Spray underside of leaves with water to dislodge mites
Step 2: Apply Abamectin 1.8 EC (0.5ml/L) miticide spray
Step 3: Rotate to Spiromesifen or Hexythiazox to prevent resistance
Step 4: Apply Neem Oil 1500 PPM as organic alternative
Step 5: Release predatory mite (Phytoseiulus persimilis) for biocontrol
Step 6: Increase irrigation frequency — mites prefer dry conditions

Tomato___Target_Spot.first_aid: Apply azoxystrobin or tebuconazole fungicide. Remove infected leaves.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove leaves with concentric ring lesions
Step 2: Apply Azoxystrobin 23 SC (1ml/L) spray
Step 3: Repeat every 7 days during tropical/humid conditions
Step 4: Alternate with Tebuconazole to prevent resistance
Step 5: Avoid dense planting — give 60cm plant spacing

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.display_name: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus (TYLCV) — Viral Disease
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.first_aid: NO CURE. Remove infected plants. Control whitefly vector with imidacloprid. Yellow sticky traps are essential.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove and burn all infected plants immediately
Step 2: Apply Imidacloprid 17.8 SL (0.5ml/L) to control whitefly vector
Step 3: Install yellow sticky traps (40/acre) for whitefly monitoring
Step 4: Apply reflective silver mulch to repel whiteflies
Step 5: Use virus-resistant tomato varieties (check with seed supplier)
Step 6: Do NOT replant tomato in same field for 2 seasons

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.first_aid: No cure. Remove infected plants. Wash hands with soap before handling plants. Control aphid vectors.
action_plan: Step 1: Remove infected plants showing mosaic mottling pattern
Step 2: Wash all handling tools with 10% bleach solution
Step 3: Control aphid vectors with Dimethoate 30 EC (1ml/L)
Step 4: Use resistant varieties — many modern hybrids have Tm gene
Step 5: Do not smoke near plants — Tobacco Mosaic Virus can transfer

Tomato___healthy.first_aid: No treatment needed. Plant is healthy!
action_plan: Continue monitoring every 3–4 days
Apply preventive Mancozeb spray before monsoon
Maintain proper calcium-boron nutrition to prevent BER
Install traps for early pest detection
